case_roles.primary_pressure: softness, ritual, silence, visual rhythm, threshold-like interaction
motion, production-method copy, conceptual metaphor, data-economy interpretation
multi-brand system, components, foundations, scalability, cohesion and differentiation

emerging_grammar_summary: The first three cases suggest a Brand3/FLOC* grammar of evidence-bound translation: abstract domains become readable through surface behavior, motion behavior, or system behavior, while claims and interpretations remain separated by confidence and review boundaries.

case_weighted.reuse_status: reusable only when anchored to visible behavior
mostly case-specific; reusable as examples of concept-bearing motion
reusable for system cases with stronger evidence standards

recurring_surface_behaviors.description: Each case translates an abstract category into a perceptual frame.
reusable_language: translates an abstract category into a legible surface system
uses a conceptual source to organize visible behavior
turns category complexity into a guided perceptual frame

recurring_surface_behaviors.description: The fixtures describe behavior before evaluating quality.
Each case implies a form of guidance: gallery threshold, seamless UI guidance, or design system guidance.

case_differences.reason: Has local visual artifacts, allowing high-confidence visual observations.
case_differences.primary_risk: Overgeneralizing softness as a Brand3/FLOC* default.

case_differences.reason: Motion and metaphor are mostly copy-based in first pass.
case_differences.primary_risk: Treating production-method copy as observed motion.

case_differences.reason: System claims need component, token, variant, or multi-screen evidence.
case_differences.primary_risk: Treating design-system claims as validated system performance.

where_schema_works: Separates facts, observations, interpretations, weak inferences, and contradictions.
Evidence anchoring prevents copy claims from silently becoming visual truth.
Confidence semantics keep copy-only interpretations out of high confidence.
Human review flags contain unsupported interpretation.
Contradictions can represent productive tensions rather than only failures.
The same schema can hold atmospheric, motion-heavy, and system-heavy cases.
where_schema_creates_friction: visual_observations currently holds both observed visual behavior and stated visual behavior.
Conceptual metaphor may deserve its own layer.
Contradictions need basis metadata such as copy_only, capture_only, copy_vs_capture, or mixed.
Human review needs per-item resolution states.
Multi-brand system records need variant or sub-brand evidence.
Corporate system cases need evidence standards for scalability, cohesion, foundations, and components.
future_report_prompt_implications: Require evidence anchors for every perceptual claim.
Require confidence semantics in prose: direct, copy-based, inferred, weak.
Ask for productive tensions, not only strengths and weaknesses.
Prevent copy-only claims from becoming confirmed visual conclusions.
Use source-status phrases such as stated by the source, visible in the surface, inferred from copy, and requires review.
Add a warning that scalability, cohesion, modularity, and component claims need multi-surface evidence.
visual_signature_review_questions: What is directly visible versus only stated in copy?
Which repeated signals create a system rather than decoration?
Is the concept visible in hierarchy, motion, components, or interaction?
Does motion reinforce a concept, or is motion only claimed?
Does a design-system claim show components, variants, tokens, or multi-screen consistency?
Where does the surface preserve difference while creating cohesion?
What productive contradiction appears between stated claim and observed signal?
Which low-confidence inferences need human review before reuse?
audit_conclusion: The most reusable layer is not any one style. It is the disciplined distinction between what the source states, what the surface shows, what Brand3 infers, and what still needs human review.
